I would say it's something I've gotten a lot more focus on, I would say in the last year is really narrowing down my focus because the times in my life where I made the most money and I grew businesses the best was when I had only one or two things going. When you have multiple things going on, your focus just gets diluted so much. And it's hard, it's hard too, because like, I might have five good things that I could be doing. And it's really hard to like take two of those good things and be like, all right, I'm not going to do these. But the reality is like to really grow that one thing, you're almost always better off just building that one thing. I kind of give that advice to Brian Hunter, like a couple months ago, I wrote about it in one of my newsletters, like he was thinking about building a supplement company and then also doing a print newsletter about how he's building the supplement company. I was like, you're better off just focusing on building a supplement company because if you actually build the supplement company correctly, you're going to make way more money than you ever will, writing a print newsletter about how to build a supplement company. So I mean, prioritizing the profitable projects is kind of always a phrase I like to keep in mind, like the ones that are actually profitable, the ones that I really want to be doing and the ones I want to spend my time on, like those are those always go to the top of the list for me. Does that help? Yeah, it does. You know, it's just kind of tricky for I guess anyone in this group because I feel like opportunities are kind of plentiful. The better you have to get at cutting stuff because yeah, there are endless opportunities. I mean, if I took up every opportunity that came my way, I'd never get anything done because I'd be trying to do 40 different things. So yeah, you kind of got to really just prioritize the one or two things that you think are the best opportunities and kind of go all in on them.
